scenario: Graceful handoff with full state transfer
description: Agent A signs shutdown event, transfers state to A'. A' presents same DID with rotation proof and a state_transfer_receipt. Expected: identity valid, trust drops to upper decay band (0.85 * 0.85 = 0.7225 at default accrual_rate=0.15), and rebuilds to 0.95 in ~11 receipts on ideal interactions. Rebuild speed is consumer-configurable via accrual_rate.

scope.evidence_basis: Author-reported, unpublished operational logs from the Sovereign Lattice (100+ agent relay sessions). All values are profile calibration for the sovereign_atom profile only; nothing in this fixture is a conformance requirement on any external specification.

expected.identity_verdict_reason: Rotation chain continuous, predecessor signed the shutdown event, state_transfer_receipt present.

rebuild_trajectory.note: trust_{n+1} = trust_n + 0.15 * (1 - trust_n) * 1.0 (ideal receipts; interaction_score = 1.0 each step).

composition.status_note: This section is an illustrative, author-proposed mapping. It imposes no requirements on AIP-13 or ERC-8004 and uses no conformance language.

aip13_delegationChain.note: Successor inherits predecessor's action envelope as a ceiling. Because trust was high pre-handoff (0.85), delegator may elect not to narrow scope on this rotation. Trust rebuilds inside the existing ceiling.

erc8004_keyRotation.note: Reputation Registry may record rotation event for audit; the trust evaluation in this profile does not depend on it.
